channel.title: SevenAtSevenPolitics
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SevenAtSevenPolitics, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Trump says he'll visit Venezuela, touts good relations with interim president
item.description: President Trump said he plans to visit Venezuela and hailed his relations with Venezuela's acting President Delcy Rodriguez.

item.title: The Folly of Switzerland’s Proposed Population Cap
item.description: Swiss voters are being asked to decide on a referendum to limit the country’s population to 10 million people.

item.title: Here Are 10 Great Justice Scalia Quotes To Mark A Decade Since His Passing
item.description: Here are some of Justice Scalia's best moments in remembrance of the 10-year anniversary of his passing.

item.title: Hispanic groups warn Trump's ICE surge could 'nullify' GOP's Texas congressional goals
item.description: Republicans may not be able to make good on the five seats they hope to net from Texas' new congressional map, according to an immigration group that ...

item.title: AI and Our Collapsing Creative Horizons
item.description: The real danger AI poses is to original thought itself.

item.title: Judge dismisses charges against men accused of assaulting ICE officer in Minneapolis shooting case
item.description: A federal judge in Minneapolis on Friday ordered the dismissal of felony assault charges against two Venezuelan men, including one shot in the leg by ...

item.title: Gen Z Is ‘Dumber’ Thanks To Adults Saturating Their Lives With Screens
item.description: Children can't be expected to dig deeper in an educational system engineered to keep them skimming.

item.title: Analog-obsessed Gen Zers are buying $40 app blockers to limit their social media use and take a break from the ‘slot machine in your pocket’
item.description: App-blocking devices like Bloom and Brick promise to improve focus and decrease compulsive phone checks by making social media harder to access.

item.title: Eileen Gu’s Terrible Choice
item.description: Gu skis for China, a choice that is a little like deciding to represent a fascist country during the 1930s.

item.title: Mamdami Demands U.S. Immigration Policy Start Obeying Islam
item.description: Mamdani invoked Islamic doctrine to define civic obligation and delegitimize lawful, constitutional authority.

item.title: Turns Out The Irishman Detained By ICE Was Facing Drug Charges Back Home
item.description: The arrest of Irishman Seamus Culleton fueled a false media narrative that ICE is grabbing law-abiding people who have done nothing wrong.

item.title: New York Fed economists confirm that Americans are the ones footing the bill for 90% of Trump’s tariffs
item.description: The president has insisted other countries are paying for his import taxes. American companies and consumers are telling a different story.

item.title: Congress Has Always Held the Power over Tariffs
item.description: The House has finally rebuked President Trump over his unilateral trade war.

item.title: Rubio-Wang Yi meeting in Munich draws limited detail despite high stakes
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio met Friday with Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi on the sidelines of the Munich Security Conference, but few details we...

item.title: Trump pardons 5 former NFL players for crimes ranging from perjury to drug trafficking
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday pardoned five former professional football players - one posthumously - for various crimes ranging from perjury to ...

item.title: Trump: ‘We don’t run from anybody’ in blasting Biden over Afghanistan withdrawal
item.description: Trump accuses Biden of leaving military equipment behind during Afghanistan withdrawal, calling it an "embarrassment" while claiming different approac...

item.title: The Week: The Nancy Guthrie Disappearance
item.description: Plus: Trump rolls back climate regulations.

item.title: Catholics, conservatives cheer ouster of ex-Miss California from WH religious panel after hijacking hearing
item.description: Former Miss California Carrie Prejean Boller ousted from Trump's Religious Liberty Commission amid controversy over her remarks at a hearing on antise...

item.title: Republicans Will Keep Losing Until They Realize Power Isn’t Evil
item.description: We can exercise power, or we can allow our enemies to exercise it. Choose wisely.

item.title: Exclusive: The Walton family-funded PE firm that owns Rapha Cycling Club presses pause on all new investments
item.description: RZC Investments, the private equity firm of Walmart heirs Tom and Steuart Walton, said it was still “actively” managing its current portfolio.

item.title: US troops blasted through steel doors 'like it was like papier-mache' to snatch Maduro, Trump says
item.description: President Donald Trump recounted the military operation that captured Venezuelan dictator Nicolás Maduro during a Fort Bragg, North Carolina, event ho...

item.title: Democrat claims SAVE Act would block married women from voting; Republicans say that's wrong
item.description: Democratic Rep. Hillary Scholten is opposing Republican SAVE America Act voter ID legislation, arguing it creates obstacles for married women voters d...

item.title: 'Doubling down on stupid': Newsom, AOC, trash Trump at European summit as they raise 2028 profiles
item.description: Democratic leaders Newsom and Ocasio-Cortez blast Trump at Munich Security Conference, positioning for potential 2028 runs while criticizing his agend...

item.title: Exclusive: Binance fires top investigators who claim to have uncovered evidence of Iranian sanctions violations
item.description: Internal findings by the fired investigators reviewed by Fortune pointed to over $1 billion in Tether flowing through Binance to Iran-linked wallets.

item.title: Top analyst warns the economy is figuring out how to grow without creating new jobs, leaving a major vulnerability
item.description: The economy will grow because of higher productivity, aided in part by AI, says Michael Pearce of Oxford Economics. But people aren't core to that sto...

item.title: Evolving Your Wealth Management Practice for 2026 and Beyond
item.description: Wealth management’s 2026 outlook: growth will hinge on transparency, integration, and relevance to women and next-generation investors. The post Evolv...

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Bank Risk
item.description: AI is exposing the limits of legacy bank controls, making governance quality critical to resilience and investor confidence. The post AI Is Reshaping ...

item.title: What Makes an Ideal Leveraged Buyout Candidate?
item.description: In competitive PE markets, successful LBOs depend on durable business models, recurring cash flows, defensible market positions, and disciplined targe...

item.title: Rethinking Household Asset Allocation Under Capital Constraints
item.description: Leveraged ETFs aren’t about more risk, they’re about using less capital to achieve a desired risk profile in household portfolios. The post Rethinking...

item.title: Stockholm’s Capital Markets Success: More Than Meatballs
item.description: Stockholm’s capital-market success reflects more than IPOs and PE activity. It offers a case study in how culture, institutions, and incentives shape ...
